experiment: GenAlign RLHF/PPO vs DPO Alignment Comparison
base_model: SFT on 50k curated conversations (10k steps, lr=2e-4)

rlhf_ppo.description: Reward model trained on preference pairs + PPO policy optimisation

dpo.description: Direct Preference Optimisation — no explicit reward model

key_findings: RLHF/PPO achieves +3.6 pp higher win-rate than DPO on hh-rlhf
DPO shows lower KL divergence from SFT (0.218 vs 0.342), preserving language quality
Both methods reduce mean toxicity by ~65% compared to SFT baseline
DPO trains 5x faster (no rollout generation) but requires an offline preference dataset
RLHF/PPO is more sensitive to KL coefficient — too high degrades reward, too low causes mode collapse
Multi-objective reward (helpfulness 0.6, safety 0.3, conciseness 0.1) further improves human evals
